channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: Tejas Mk1A Induction Nears as HAL Upgrades VVIP Facilities Ahead of High-Profile Handover Ceremony
item.description: Hindustan Aeronautics Limited’s latest tender (HAL/LCA-Tejas/PM/C-03/2026) for wall panelling at the VVIP Dining Hall inside the Customer Service Building at its Bengaluru Complex may appear minor in scale, but its timing and location point to a much larger strategic context. Issued by the LCA Tejas Division as an open works contract with an estimated value of ?16.25 lakh, the project is part of a broader infrastructure refinement effort underway at HAL’s Plant-I facility, which serves as the nerve center for India’s indigenous fighter program.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Indian Navy Targets “Unjammable” Radar with 25 Crore Quantum Tech Challenge
item.description: The Indian Navy has initiated one of its most ambitious technology programs to date with the launch of the “Sovereign Quantum Radar” challenge under the ADITI 4.0 (Acing Development of Innovative Technologies with iDEX) framework on March 17, 2026. Positioned as a true “moonshot” project, the initiative aims to fundamentally redefine how targets are detected in contested environments, particularly as conventional radar systems approach their operational limits against modern stealth technologies.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: 56-Fold Growth Propels India into Global Arms Export Race, Rivaling Russia: Russian Analyst
item.description: Russian journalist Vasily Golovnin has highlighted a remarkable shift in the international defense landscape. Once one of the world’s largest importers of Soviet and Russian weaponry, India has rapidly transformed into a significant arms exporter, now actively competing with Moscow even in regions long considered Russia’s stronghold.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Tejas Mk II Scale Model Appears at Western Air Command: Visual Signal of India’s Next-Generation Fighter Momentum
item.description: A striking scale model of India’s next-generation Light Combat Aircraft, the Tejas MkII, has been installed at the Western Air Command of the Indian Air Force, signalling strong institutional momentum behind the indigenous fighter programme.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: R-37M Missile to Deliver Significant Operational Edge to Indian Air Force in South Asia : Indian Analyst
item.description: India’s reported interest in the Russian R-37M long-range air-to-air missile is set to reshape aerial combat dynamics in the region, offering a powerful counter to Pakistan’s growing fleet of Chinese fighters and bolstering deterrence along the borders with both Pakistan and China, according to leading defence experts.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India’s Su-30K Decision Revisited: How a 2007 “Fleet Cleanup” May Have Cost DRDO Its Ideal Fighter Testbed
item.description: The Indian Air Force’s 2007 decision to return its initial batch of 18 Su-30K fighters to Russia has long been viewed as a pragmatic step toward fleet standardization around the far more capable Su-30MKI. At the time, the logic was straightforward: the Su-30K lacked thrust vectoring, modern avionics, and the Indo-Western systems integration that defined the MKI, making an upgrade economically unattractive. However, with India now accelerating indigenous engine and avionics development, that decision increasingly looks like a missed opportunity—specifically in the context of creating a dedicated, fighter-class flying testbed.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India’s Strategic Opportunity: Offer Akash Air Defense Systems to GCC Nations Like UAE for Real-World Evaluation
item.description: As fragile ceasefires hold across key conflict zones in the Middle East, India has a timely chance to strengthen its defense diplomacy in the Gulf. By proactively offering the Akash air defense system to partners such as the United Arab Emirates on a loan or field-evaluation basis, New Delhi can accelerate its emergence as a major global arms exporter while delivering immediate strategic value to regional allies facing evolving aerial threats.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Commander of US Pacific Air Forces visits India to strengthen bilateral defence cooperation
item.description: The Commander of U.S. Pacific Air Forces General Kevin Schneider visited India April 19-25, emphasizing the United States’ commitment to deepening its strategic defense partnership with India and advancing a shared vision for a secure and stable Indo-Pacific.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: NNC/FGN opposes IAF radar installation at Pfutsero
item.description: The NNC/FGN, under the leadership of Zhopra Vero, has opposed the proposal by the Indian Air Force (IAF) to establish a mountain radar system at Pfutsero in Chakhesang region, stating that the move is unacceptable without the consent of the Naga people.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Unravelling of 22 minutes, then their command collapsed: Indian Army on Op Sindoor
item.description: The Indian Army on Monday shared a post on social media highlighting how Operation Sindoor disrupted the command structure of the terrorists in just 22 minutes. “Resolve. Clarity. Mission Focus. Justice Served — Swift and Precise. Evidence doesn’t argue – It settles,” the Army wrote on X. This post comes just days before the first anniversary of the Indian Army’s decisive military action — Operation Sindoor.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Raksha Mantri to lead Indian delegation at SCO Defence Ministers’ Meeting in Bishkek, Kyrgyzstan
item.description: Raksha Mantri Shri Rajnath Singh will lead a high-level Indian delegation to the Shanghai Cooperation Organisation (SCO) Defence Ministers’ Meeting which will be held in Bishkek, Kyrgyzstan on April 28, 2026. During the meeting, the Defence Ministers of various member countries will deliberate upon a number of issues concerning the defence & security of the region. Issues related to international peace, counter-terrorism and defence cooperation among SCO member states will also be discussed.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Foreign Secretary Misri likely to visit Nepal next month: sources
item.description: Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri is likely to travel to Nepal next month, the first high-level visit from India after the formation of the Balendra Shah-led government in March, official sources here said on Monday. The visit is expected to take place in the first half of May, the official sources at Nepal’s Foreign Affairs Ministry said. The exact date and details of the visit are not yet finalised.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Salim Dola’s Arrest Hits ISI Revenue Stream: India Plans Two-Step Extradition Via UAE
item.description: The arrest of Salim Dola, who ran a Rs 5,000 crore drug empire, is a major blow to the Dawood Ibrahim Syndicate. Dola was arrested in Turkey in a joint operation by the National Intelligence Organisation and police units.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: From poll violence to terror supply chains: West Bengal’s crude bomb industry under NIA scanner
item.description: The bomb factories in West Bengal are back in the spotlight following the recovery of 79 crude bombs at Bhangar in South 24 Parganas district. The probe has been taken over by the National Investigation Agency (NIA) and the central agency would go into the larger picture as this does not appear to be an isolated incident.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: School declared ‘unlawful entity’ in J-K’s Shopian; institute chairman denies link with any illegal outfit
item.description: A school allegedly run by individuals affiliated with a banned organisation in Shopian district of Jammu and Kashmir has been declared an “unlawful entity” under the Unlawful Activities (Prevention) Act (UAPA). Kashmir Divisional Commissioner Anshul Garg issued a two-page order based on a dossier presented by Shopian SSP, pointing towards the alleged illegal activities at Darul Uloom Jamia Sirajul Uloom at Imam Sahib in Shopian.
item.dc:creator: admin
